#7D8AFF40"></div><div class="MuiContainer-root MuiContainer-maxWidthMd mui-style-mc1cng"><div><div class="MuiBox-root mui-style-l81d87"><p>Kimi K2.6 is Moonshot AI’s latest flagship open-source model, released on April 20, 2026 under a Modified MIT license. It is a native multimodal agentic model built on a 1-trillion parameter Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture, with 32 billion parameters activated per token. The model is designed for long-horizon coding, autonomous execution, and multi-agent orchestration, and is available via the DeepInfra API as <strong>moonshotai/Kimi-K2.6</strong>.</p>



<h2 class=""><strong>Key Capabilities</strong></h2>



<p><strong>Agent Swarm and Multi-Agent Orchestration</strong></p>



<p>Kimi K2.6 includes an Agent Swarm system that scales to 300 domain-specialized sub-agents, executing up to 4,000 coordinated steps in a single autonomous run — up from 100 sub-agents and 1,500 steps in K2.5. The orchestration layer decomposes complex prompts into parallel subtasks and synthesizes outputs into finished deliverables such as research documents, functional websites, or spreadsheets.</p>



<p><strong>Coding and Full-Stack Development</strong></p>



<p>The model is optimized for software engineering across Rust, Go, and Python, handling tasks from front-end generation to DevOps and performance optimization. Its coding-driven design capability transforms text prompts and visual mockups into production-ready interfaces. Note: image input is not exposed through the API — vision capabilities are used internally by the model’s MoonViT encoder but are not available as a direct API input parameter.</p>



<p><strong>Long-Horizon Autonomous Execution</strong></p>



<p>Kimi K2.6 supports persistent background agent execution, including continuous runs of 12+ hours with thousands of tool calls. It is designed for cross-platform operations and multi-step workflows that extend well beyond standard chat interaction patterns.</p>



<h2 class=""><strong>Technical Specifications and Performance</strong></h2>



<p><strong>Architecture</strong></p>



<ul class="">
<li><strong>Model type:</strong> Mixture-of-Experts (MoE), 1 trillion total parameters</li>



<li><strong>Active parameters:</strong> 32 billion per token</li>



<li><strong>Experts:</strong> 384 total, 8 selected per token (plus 1 shared expert), 61 layers</li>



<li><strong>Context window:</strong> 262,144 tokens (256K), using Multi-Head Latent Attention (MLA)</li>



<li><strong>Vision encoder:</strong> 400M-parameter MoonViT (used internally; image input not exposed via API)</li>



<li><strong>Quantization:</strong> Native INT4 and FP4 supported for high-concurrency deployment</li>



<li><strong>Inference engines:</strong> vLLM, SGLang, KTransformers</li>
</ul>



<p><strong>Benchmark Performance</strong></p>



<p>Kimi K2.6 leads on agentic and coding benchmarks, while trailing on pure math reasoning. All Kimi K2.6 results use thinking mode enabled. Asterisked (*) competitor scores were re-evaluated by Moonshot under the same conditions, as published scores were not available from the original sources.</p>



<table><thead><tr><th><strong>Category</strong></th><th><strong>Benchmark</strong></th><th><strong>Kimi K2.6</strong></th><th><strong>GPT-5.4</strong></th><th><strong>Claude Opus 4.6</strong></th><th><strong>Gemini 3.1 Pro</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Agentic</td><td>HLE-Full (w/ tools)</td><td>54.0</td><td>52.1</td><td>53.0</td><td>51.4</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>DeepSearchQA (Acc)</td><td>83.0</td><td>63.7</td><td>80.6</td><td>60.2</td></tr><tr><td>Coding</td><td>SWE-Bench Pro</td><td>58.6</td><td>57.7</td><td>53.4</td><td>54.2</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>LiveCodeBench v6</td><td>89.6</td><td>—</td><td>88.8</td><td>91.7</td></tr><tr><td>Reasoning</td><td>AIME 2026</td><td>96.4</td><td>99.2</td><td>96.7*</td><td>98.3*</td></tr><tr><td>Vision</td><td>MathVision (w/ Py)</td><td>93.2</td><td>96.1*</td><td>84.6*</td><td>95.7*</td></tr></tbody></table>



<p>Additional results:</p>



<ul class="">
<li><strong>SWE-Bench Verified:</strong> 80.2 — within 0.6 points of Claude Opus 4.6 (80.8)</li>



<li><strong>BrowseComp:</strong> 83.2 in single-agent mode; 86.3 in Agent Swarm mode</li>



<li><strong>IMO-AnswerBench:</strong> 86.0, ahead of Claude Opus 4.6 (75.3)</li>



<li><strong>Pure reasoning gap:</strong> GPT-5.4 leads on AIME 2026 (99.2 vs 96.4) and GPQA-Diamond (92.8 vs 90.5). For workloads requiring high single-turn mathematical reasoning accuracy, this gap is relevant.</li>
</ul>



<h2 class=""><strong>Getting Started with the Kimi K2.6 API</strong></h2>



<p>Kimi K2.6 is available via DeepInfra’s OpenAI-compatible API.</p>



<p><strong>Authentication</strong></p>



<ul class="">
<li>Obtain your API key from your DeepInfra account dashboard.</li>



<li>Include it in requests as a Bearer token: <strong>Authorization: Bearer YOUR_DEEPINFRA_API_KEY</strong></li>
</ul>



<p><strong>API Endpoint Basics</strong></p>



<ul class="">
<li><strong>Base URL:</strong> https://api.deepinfra.com/v1/openai/chat/completions</li>



<li><strong>Model identifier:</strong> moonshotai/Kimi-K2.6</li>



<li><strong>HTTP method:</strong> POST</li>



<li><strong>Content type:</strong> application/json</li>
</ul>



<p><strong>cURL Example</strong></p>



<div><span tabindex="0"></span><pre tabindex="0"><code class="hljs language-arduino">curl -X POST \
  https:<span class="hljs-comment">//api.deepinfra.com/v1/openai/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_DEEPINFRA_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{</span>
    <span class="hljs-string">"model"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"moonshotai/Kimi-K2.6"</span>,
    <span class="hljs-string">"messages"</span>: [
      {<span class="hljs-string">"role"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"system"</span>, <span class="hljs-string">"content"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"You are a helpful assistant."</span>},
      {<span class="hljs-string">"role"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"user"</span>, <span class="hljs-string">"content"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"What are the core capabilities of Kimi K2.6?"</span>}
    ],
    <span class="hljs-string">"max_tokens"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">150</span>,
    <span class="hljs-string">"temperature"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0.7</span>
  }'</code><div class="copy-button">copy</div></pre></div>



<p><strong>Python Example</strong></p>



<div><span tabindex="0"></span><pre tabindex="0"><code class="hljs language-python"><span class="hljs-keyword">import</span> requests
<span class="hljs-keyword">import</span> json


API_KEY = <span class="hljs-string">"YOUR_DEEPINFRA_API_KEY"</span>
API_URL = <span class="hljs-string">"https://api.deepinfra.com/v1/openai/chat/completions"</span>


headers = {
    <span class="hljs-string">"Authorization"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">f"Bearer <span class="hljs-subst">{API_KEY}</span>"</span>,
    <span class="hljs-string">"Content-Type"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"application/json"</span>
}


payload = {
    <span class="hljs-string">"model"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"moonshotai/Kimi-K2.6"</span>,
    <span class="hljs-string">"messages"</span>: [
        {<span class="hljs-string">"role"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"system"</span>, <span class="hljs-string">"content"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"You are a helpful assistant."</span>},
        {<span class="hljs-string">"role"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"user"</span>, <span class="hljs-string">"content"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"What are the core capabilities of Kimi K2.6?"</span>}
    ],
    <span class="hljs-string">"max_tokens"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">150</span>,
    <span class="hljs-string">"temperature"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0.7</span>
}


<span class="hljs-keyword">try</span>:
    response = requests.post(API_URL, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(payload))
    response.raise_for_status()
    <span class="hljs-built_in">print</span>(json.dumps(response.json(), indent=<span class="hljs-number">2</span>))
<span class="hljs-keyword">except</span> requests.exceptions.RequestException <span class="hljs-keyword">as</span> e:
    <span class="hljs-built_in">print</span>(<span class="hljs-string">f"API request failed: <span class="hljs-subst">{e}</span>"</span>)</code><div class="copy-button">copy</div></pre></div>



<h2 class=""><strong>API Parameters and Response Format</strong></h2>



<p><strong>Common Parameters</strong></p>



<table><thead><tr><th><strong>Parameter</strong></th><th><strong>Type</strong></th><th><strong>Description</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>model</td><td>string</td><td>Required. Use moonshotai/Kimi-K2.6.</td></tr><tr><td>messages</td><td>array</td><td>Required. The conversation history (system, user, assistant).</td></tr><tr><td>max_tokens</td><td>integer</td><td>Optional. Limits the length of the generated output.</td></tr><tr><td>temperature</td><td>number</td><td>Optional. Controls randomness (0.0 to 2.0).</td></tr><tr><td>stream</td><td>boolean</td><td>Optional. If true, sends partial deltas as server-sent events.</td></tr></tbody></table>



<p><strong>Response Format</strong></p>



<p>A successful request returns a JSON object. Key fields: <strong>choices[0].message.content</strong> contains the generated text; <strong>usage</strong> contains token counts for billing.</p>



<div><span tabindex="0"></span><pre tabindex="0"><code class="hljs language-json"><span class="hljs-punctuation">{</span>
  <span class="hljs-attr">"id"</span><span class="hljs-punctuation">:</span> <span class="hljs-string">"chatcmpl-xxx"</span><span class="hljs-punctuation">,</span>
  <span class="hljs-attr">"model"</span><span class="hljs-punctuation">:</span> <span class="hljs-string">"moonshotai/Kimi-K2.6"</span><span class="hljs-punctuation">,</span>
  <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span><span class="hljs-punctuation">:</span> <span class="hljs-punctuation">[</span>
    <span class="hljs-punctuation">{</span>
      <span class="hljs-attr">"message"</span><span class="hljs-punctuation">:</span> <span class="hljs-punctuation">{</span>
        <span class="hljs-attr">"role"</span><span class="hljs-punctuation">:</span> <span class="hljs-string">"assistant"</span><span class="hljs-punctuation">,</span>
        <span class="hljs-attr">"content"</span><span class="hljs-punctuation">:</span> <span class="hljs-string">"Kimi K2.6 is a multimodal agentic model..."</span>
      <span class="hljs-punctuation">}</span><span class="hljs-punctuation">,</span>
      <span class="hljs-attr">"finish_reason"</span><span class="hljs-punctuation">:</span> <span class="hljs-string">"stop"</span>
    <span class="hljs-punctuation">}</span>
  <span class="hljs-punctuation">]</span><span class="hljs-punctuation">,</span>
  <span class="hljs-attr">"usage"</span><span class="hljs-punctuation">:</span> <span class="hljs-punctuation">{</span>
    <span class="hljs-attr">"prompt_tokens"</span><span class="hljs-punctuation">:</span> <span class="hljs-number">25</span><span class="hljs-punctuation">,</span>
    <span class="hljs-attr">"completion_tokens"</span><span class="hljs-punctuation">:</span> <span class="hljs-number">130</span><span class="hljs-punctuation">,</span>
    <span class="hljs-attr">"total_tokens"</span><span class="hljs-punctuation">:</span> <span class="hljs-number">155</span>
  <span class="hljs-punctuation">}</span>
<span class="hljs-punctuation">}</span></code><div class="copy-button">copy</div></pre></div>

<table><thead><tr><th><strong>Token Type</strong></th><th><strong>Price per 1M Tokens</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Input Tokens</td><td>$0.75</td></tr><tr><td>Output Tokens</td><td>$3.50</td></tr><tr><td>Cached Input Tokens</td><td>$0.15</td></tr></tbody></table>



<p>For the most current information on pricing, visit the <a href="https://deepinfra.com/pricing">DeepInfra Pricing Page</a>.</p>



<h2 class=""><strong>Conclusion</strong></h2>



<p>Kimi K2.6 is a capable open-weight agentic model that leads on long-horizon coding and multi-agent orchestration benchmarks while remaining competitive with closed-source frontier models on key software engineering tasks. Its 262K context window, Agent Swarm architecture, and open weights under a Modified MIT license give teams both performance and deployment flexibility — including self-hosting on vLLM or SGLang. The model’s main trade-offs are a smaller context window than some proprietary alternatives (1M vs 262K), no native image input via the API, and trailing scores on pure math reasoning benchmarks relative to GPT-5.4.</p>



<p>To start building with Kimi K2.6, explore the <a href="https://deepinfra.com/docs">DeepInfra API documentation</a> or visit the <a href="https://www.kimi.com/blog/kimi-k2-6">Moonshot AI tech blog</a> for the full technical report.</p>

It is a native multimodal agentic model built on a 1-trillion parameter Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture, with 32 billion parameters activated per token. The model is designed for long-horizon coding, autonomous execution, and multi-agent orchestration, and is available via the DeepInfra API as \u003cstrong\u003emoonshotai/Kimi-K2.6\u003c/strong\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003ch2 class=\"wp-block-heading\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eKey Capabilities\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003cp class=\"wp-block-paragraph\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAgent Swarm and Multi-Agent Orchestration\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003cp class=\"wp-block-paragraph\"\u003eKimi K2.6 includes an Agent Swarm system that scales to 300 domain-specialized sub-agents, executing up to 4,000 coordinated steps in a single autonomous run — up from 100 sub-agents and 1,500 steps in K2.5. The orchestration layer decomposes complex prompts into parallel subtasks and synthesizes outputs into finished deliverables such as research documents, functional websites, or spreadsheets.\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003cp class=\"wp-block-paragraph\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCoding and Full-Stack Development\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003cp class=\"wp-block-paragraph\"\u003eThe model is optimized for software engineering across Rust, Go, and Python, handling tasks from front-end generation to DevOps and performance optimization. Its coding-driven design capability transforms text prompts and visual mockups into production-ready interfaces. Note: image input is not exposed through the API — vision capabilities are used internally by the model\u0026#8217;s MoonViT encoder but are not available as a direct API input parameter.\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003cp class=\"wp-block-paragraph\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eLong-Horizon Autonomous Execution\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003cp class=\"wp-block-paragraph\"\u003eKimi K2.6 supports persistent background agent execution, including continuous runs of 12+ hours with thousands of tool calls. It is designed for cross-platform operations and multi-step workflows that extend well beyond standard chat interaction patterns.\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003ch2 class=\"wp-block-heading\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTechnical Specifications and Performance\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003cp class=\"wp-block-paragraph\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eArchitecture\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003cul class=\"wp-block-list\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eModel type:\u003c/strong\u003e Mixture-of-Experts (MoE), 1 trillion total parameters\u003c/li\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eActive parameters:\u003c/strong\u003e 32 billion per token\u003c/li\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eExperts:\u003c/strong\u003e 384 total, 8 selected per token (plus 1 shared expert), 61 layers\u003c/li\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eContext window:\u003c/strong\u003e 262,144 tokens (256K), using Multi-Head Latent Attention (MLA)\u003c/li\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eVision encoder:\u003c/strong\u003e 400M-parameter MoonViT (used internally; image input not exposed via API)\u003c/li\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQuantization:\u003c/strong\u003e Native INT4 and FP4 supported for high-concurrency deployment\u003c/li\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eInference engines:\u003c/strong\u003e vLLM, SGLang, KTransformers\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003cp class=\"wp-block-paragraph\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBenchmark Performance\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003cp class=\"wp-block-paragraph\"\u003eKimi K2.6 leads on agentic and coding benchmarks, while trailing on pure math reasoning. All Kimi K2.6 results use thinking mode enabled. Asterisked (*) competitor scores were re-evaluated by Moonshot under the same conditions, as published scores were not available from the original sources.\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003cfigure class=\"wp-block-table\"\u003e\u003ctable class=\"has-fixed-layout\"\u003e\u003cthead\u003e\u003ctr\u003e\u003cth\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCategory\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/th\u003e\u003cth\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBenchmark\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/th\u003e\u003cth\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eKimi K2.6\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/th\u003e\u003cth\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eGPT-5.4\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/th\u003e\u003cth\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eClaude Opus leads on long-horizon coding and multi-agent orchestration benchmarks while remaining competitive with closed-source frontier models on key software engineering tasks. The model\u0026#8217;s main trade-offs are a smaller context window than some proprietary alternatives (1M vs 262K), no native image input via the API, and trailing scores on pure math reasoning benchmarks relative to GPT-5.4.\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\n\n\u003cp class=\"wp-block-paragraph\"\u003eTo start building with Kimi K2.6, explore the \u003ca href=\"https://deepinfra.com/docs\"\u003eDeepInfra API documentation\u003c/a\u003e or visit the \u003ca href=\"https://www.kimi.com/blog/kimi-k2-6\"\u003eMoonshot AI tech blog\u003c/a\u003e for the full technical report.\u003c/p\u003e\n","excerpt":"\u003cp\u003eKimi K2.6 is Moonshot AI\u0026#8217;s latest flagship open-source model, released on April 20, 2026 under a Modified MIT license.
